channel.title: Water-Transport on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Water-Transport on emsenn.net

item.title: Transpiration
item.description: Transpiration: the evaporative loss of water vapor from plant surfaces, primarily through open stomata in leaves and green stems. While small amounts of water escape directly through the cuticle (cuticular transpiration), stomatal transpiration dominates — a single large tree can transpire 200 to 400 liters of water per day, an amount equal to the annual rainfall in some regions. Transpiration is thus a massive water flux through plants, far exceeding the water needed for growth or metabolic reactions.
